urgency.message: 228 critical CVE(s) and 2 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: 3 Paths to Upgrade Windows 11 before 24H2 End of Servicing (EOL)
items.summary: Key Takeaways Windows 11 24H2 reaches the end of servicing on October 13, 2026, making timely enterprise upgrades critical. Enterprises often face version drift, with multiple Windows 11 builds across endpoints requiring different upgrade paths. Upgrade approaches vary based on system state and may

items.title: Lookalike npm Package Hides a Multi-Stage Windows RAT
items.summary: JFrog found an npm package impersonating postcss-selector-parser to drop a multi-stage Windows RAT

items.title: GitHub Updates actions/checkout to Block Common Pwn Request Attack Patterns
items.summary:
GitHub is moving to strengthen software supply chain security by updating "actions/checkout" to block pwn request attacks that exploit the risky use of the "pull_request_target workflow" trigger to run malicious code with the workflow's full privileges.

Effective June 18, 2026, the latest version o

items.title: OpenAI Expands Daybreak to Help Defenders Patch Flaws
items.summary: OpenAI expanded Daybreak with a full GPT-5.5-Cyber release to help defenders patch software flaws

items.title: The Exploit Doesn't Exist. You Can Still Prove It Works Against You
items.summary: Attackers can now weaponize newly disclosed vulnerabilities far faster than most organizations can patch them. Picus Security explains how security teams can validate exploitability before a public exploit even exists. [...]

items.summary: Updated an acknowledgement. This is an informational change only.

items.summary: Updated an acknowledgement. This is an informational change only.

items.title: Trump Issues Executive Order to Fast-Track Post-Quantum Migration
items.summary: All US federal agencies will have to complete their post-quantum cryptography transition by 2031, according to a new Trump Executive Order

items.title: LastPass confirms data breach in Klue supply chain attack
items.summary: LastPass announced that hackers accessed customer data from its Salesforce environment after stealing the company's OAuth tokens in the Klue supply chain attack earlier this month. [...]

items.title: SocGholish Takedown Highlights Malicious TDS Threats
items.summary: SocGholish uses traffic distribution systems (TDSs) to provide initial access into victims' networks for cybercrime groups such as the notorious Evil Corp.

items.title: What the Miasma campaign reveals about the new supply chain threat model and the underground market for developer credentials
items.summary: A stolen session cookie sat in underground markets for seven weeks before attackers used it to poison 32 Red Hat packages in the npm software registry, an example of the industrial approach behind modern supply chain attacks.Key takeawaysMiasma is a self-propagating npm worm derived from Mini Shai-H

items.title: Two Scattered Spider members plead guilty over cyberattack that crippled London transit
items.summary: A 20-year-old and an 18-year-old admitted to infiltrating the network of Transport for London in 2024, disrupting public transportation services for months.

items.title: Eight-Year-Old Samsung KNOX Flaw Exposed Millions of Galaxy Devices to Kernel Attacks
items.summary:
The high-severity use-after-free vulnerability in Samsung's KNOX security framework affected Android-powered Galaxy devices from the S9 through S25.
The post Eight-Year-Old Samsung KNOX Flaw Exposed Millions of Galaxy Devices to Kernel Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: GTA 6 Scams Emerge as Pre-Orders Open
items.summary: Cybercriminals launch fake GTA 6 pre-order sites offering early access for crypto payments

items.title: CISO Conversations: Carl Froggett – Combining CISO and CIO at Deep Instinct
items.summary:
Carl Froggett combines CISO and CIO. He currently occupies both positions at Deep Instinct. Before then, he was CISO at Citi for almost 17 years.
The post CISO Conversations: Carl Froggett – Combining CISO and CIO at Deep Instinct appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FortiBleed Attackers Turn Firewalls Into Credential Stealers as Heists Persist
items.summary: The threat actors engineered a Golang-based sniffer to target 430,000 FortiGate firewalls and identify 110 million credentials in the ongoing global campaign.

items.title: Webinar: Why email security teams are drowning in alerts
items.summary: Phishing, BEC, and account takeover attacks continue to overwhelm security teams with alerts and investigations. This webinar explores how behavioral AI can help automate detection and response workflows, reducing alert fatigue and improving operational efficiency. [...]

items.title: Algerian Man Extradited to US for Running Cybercrime Marketplaces
items.summary:
26-year-old Abdellah Belmili faces up to 30 years in prison for allegedly operating the marketplaces Market0Day and Spoxy.
The post Algerian Man Extradited to US for Running Cybercrime Marketplaces appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FFmpeg PixelSmash Flaw Allows RCE on Video Players, Media Servers, NAS Appliances
items.summary:
Attackers can send crafted media files to execute code in any application that uses FFmpeg’s libavcodec library.
The post FFmpeg PixelSmash Flaw Allows RCE on Video Players, Media Servers, NAS Appliances appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: OpenAI Refocuses Cybersecurity Efforts on Patching Over Discovery
items.summary:
OpenAI has expanded its Daybreak cybersecurity initiative with a new suite of tools and partnerships.
The post OpenAI Refocuses Cybersecurity Efforts on Patching Over Discovery appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Russian Initial Access Broker Behind FortiBleed Campaign
items.summary:
Using a custom sniffer, the threat actor has captured over 110 million credentials since at least February 2026.
The post Russian Initial Access Broker Behind FortiBleed Campaign appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Scattered Spider Teens Convicted of TfL Cyber-Attack
items.summary: Two young British men have pleaded guilty to hacking Transport for London as part of a Scattered Spider plot

items.title: Canadian Electricity Provider London Hydro Discloses Data Breach
items.summary:
Hackers stole customers’ names, addresses, email addresses, phone numbers, and account information.
The post Canadian Electricity Provider London Hydro Discloses Data Breach appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Malicious npm Packages Pose as PostCSS Tools to Deliver Windows RAT
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a set of malicious npm packages that are designed to deliver a Windows-based remote access trojan (RAT).

The list of identified packages, is below -


  aes-decode-runner-pro (145 downloads)
  postcss-minify-selector (256 downloads)
  postcss-minify-selecto

items.title: Trump Signs Executive Order Accelerating Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration
items.summary:
Federal agencies are required to transition high-value assets and high-impact systems to use PQC by the end of 2030 and 2031.
The post Trump Signs Executive Order Accelerating Post-Quantum Cryptography Migration  appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.summary: The Five Eyes Alliance has published a rare call to action for organizations facing AI threats

items.summary:
Direct messages sent via WhatsApp are being used to distribute malicious Visual Basic Script (VBScript) files that lead to the installation of legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software.

Per findings from Kaspersky, the active campaign is targeting users of WhatsApp Desktop and What

items.summary:
OpenAI on Monday said it's releasing an improved version of its GPT‑5.5‑Cyber model to trusted defenders as part of the Daybreak initiative the artificial intelligence (AI) company announced last month.

items.title: The Global Namespace Risk: Universal Bucket Hijacking Technique for Cloud Data Exfiltration
items.summary:
Unit 42 research details how attackers could exploit global name uniqueness in bucket hijacking to redirect cloud data streams across major CSPs.

items.title: JaredFromSubway MEV bot hacked in $15 million crypto theft
items.summary: The JaredFromSubway Ethereum MEV (Maximal Extractable Value) bot suffered a $15 million loss after an attacker manipulated the opportunity-detection logic by creating fake cryptocurrency trading opportunities. [...]

items.title: DifyTap Bugs Let Attackers 'Wiretap' AI Chat Histories
items.summary: Four vulnerabilities allow attackers to exploit Dify, a platform for AI application building and management, to silently access and exfiltrate sensitive data.

items.title: FFmpeg fixes PixelSmash flaw in widely used video decoder
items.summary: A newly disclosed FFmpeg flaw dubbed 'PixelSmash' could be exploited for remote code execution on Jellyfin servers under certain conditions, and can also trigger a denial-of-service  condition in applications like Kodi, Emby, Nextcloud, PhotoPrism, and OBS Studio. [...]

items.title: FortiBleed campaign used custom FortiGate sniffer to steal credentials
items.summary: Security firm SOCRadar says the large-scale FortiBleed campaign targeting Fortinet FortiGate devices used custom sniffers to harvest authentication secrets from compromised firewalls and steal credentials. [...]

items.title: ShapedPlugin WordPress Pro Plugins Backdoored in Supply Chain Attack
items.summary:
Multiple WordPress plugins from ShapedPlugin were compromised in a supply chain attack after unknown threat actors managed to tamper with the official release channels and push backdoor code.

items.title: Microsoft says Windows 11 26H2 is coming soon, details upgrade process
items.summary: Microsoft has confirmed that Windows 11 version 26H2 will be the next feature update and that devices running Windows 11 24H2 and 25H2 will be able to upgrade using a small enablement package. [...]

items.title: OpenAI Launches Full-Scale Effort to Patch Open-Source Bugs as It Takes on Anthropic’s Mythos
items.summary: Amid concerns about AI models’ cybersecurity capabilities, OpenAI revealed an improved version of GPT-5.5-Cyber and its “Patch the Planet” initiative to fix open-source software bugs.

items.title: 29-Year-Old Squid Proxy Bug 'Squidbleed' Can Leak Cleartext HTTP Requests
items.summary:
A heap over-read in the Squid web proxy can leak another user's cleartext HTTP request, including any credentials or session tokens it carries, to anyone already allowed to send traffic through the same proxy.

The bug traces to a 1997 FTP-parsing change and is still live in Squid's default configur

items.title: Researchers Detail DifyTap Flaws in Dify That Could Expose AI Chats Across Tenants

items.title: Crypto Heist Fueled by Elaborate Fake Reputation-Boosting Campaign
items.summary: Attackers are using multiple online channels — including GitHub, YouTube, and VirusTotal — to build an illusion of trust to spread a cross-platform clipboard hijacker.

items.summary: ESET details GentleKiller, the EDR-killer framework the Gentlemen ransomware gang gives affiliates

items.title: He Thought He Was Secure; His Phone Number Got Stolen Anyway
items.summary: Threat actors can easily steal one-time passwords sent by text when they conduct a SIM swap attack. This can lead to account takeovers, so users must layer up their security measures.

items.title: Suspected cyberattack triggers false emergency alerts across parts of Brazil
items.summary: The incident occurred early Saturday when at least a dozen unauthorized alerts were sent through Brazil's Civil Defense Alert system, a platform designed to warn residents about imminent threats such as floods, landslides and other natural disasters.

items.title: Unpatchable BootROM Flaw Impacts Apple A12, A13 Chips
items.summary: Apple BootROM exploit exposes unpatchable USB flaw on A12 and A13 devices
